title: 10 best herbal tinctures for eye infection
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for eye infection are concentrated liquid extracts of healing herbs, which are used to treat various eye ailments. They are a natural and safe alternative to prescription medications, with fewer side effects. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with eye infection include Eyebright, Goldenseal, and Calendula. Eyebright contains anti-inflammatory and astringent properties that reduce redness, swelling, and irritation. Goldenseal has antibacterial and antimicrobial properties that help fight infection and promote healing. Calendula has anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties that soothe irritation and promote tissue regeneration. By using these herbal tinctures, individuals can effectively treat eye infections, reduce discomfort, and improve their overall quality of life.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright tincture helps with eye infection because it contains anti-inflammatory, astringent, and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation, irritation, and redness in the eyes. The herb's active compounds, such as tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, can help soothe irritated eyes and promote healing. Eyebright tincture can also help reduce excess fluid and mucus production in the eyes, providing relief from symptoms such as watery and itchy eyes. Its potent antimicrobial properties can help fight off bacterial and viral infections, making it an effective natural remedy for treating various eye conditions.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eyebright tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituents like flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps reduce eye inflammation associated with eye infections.
Astringent: The astringent property of eyebright tincture, attributed to tannins, helps constrict and tighten eye tissues, reducing discharge and providing relief from eye infections.
Antimicrobial: Eyebright tincture's antimicrobial property, owing to its active components like phenolic acids, flavonoids, and essential oils, aids in inhibiting bacterial growth in eye infections.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of eyebright tincture, attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, helps protect the eye from oxidative stress and damage caused by eye infections.
Analgesic: Eyebright tincture's analgesic property, due to the presence of active constituents like tannins and alkaloids, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Crush 2 ounces of dried eyebright herb using a mortar and pestle, then transfer it to a clean quart-sized jar.
Pour 16 ounces of vodka or rum over the crushed herb, ensuring it is fully submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently once a day.
Strain the tincture through a cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Label the bottle with the name, ingredients, and date, then store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in active compounds, the flowers are used in tinctures for their anti-inflammatory and astringent properties, beneficial for treating eye infections.
Leaves: High in therapeutic constituents, the leaves are often used in tinctures due to their antimicrobial and soothing effects, which help alleviate eye inflammation and irritation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain tincture helps with eye infection because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The herb contains compounds such as allantoin, mucilage, and tannins that can soothe irritation, reduce inflammation, and fight off bacterial or viral infections. Plantain tincture can help relieve symptoms of eye infections such as redness, itching, burning, and discharge. Its astringent properties can also help shrink swollen tissues and promote healing of the eye. Additionally, plantain has been shown to have a protective effect on the cornea, making it an effective natural remedy for eye infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Plantain tincture contains compounds like aucubin, which help inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, making it effective in treating eye infections caused by pathogens.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain tincture has active constituents such as allantoin, which reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes, providing relief from symptoms associated with eye infections.
Antioxidant properties: Plantain tincture contains phenolic acids and flavonoids that exhibit antioxidant properties, helping protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage caused by infection.
Astringent properties: The tannins present in plantain tincture act as an astringent, helping to tighten and tone eye tissues, reducing discharge, and promoting healing in eye infections.
Demulcent properties: Plantain tincture contains mucilage, which forms a soothing film over the eyes, providing a protective barrier and reducing irritation and discomfort caused by eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest plantain leaves from a healthy, organic source, choosing young, fully expanded leaves for optimal potency.
Chop or grind 2 ounces of fresh plantain leaves and place them in a clean, dry jar, then add 1 cup of 80-100 proof vodka or rum, ensuring the leaves are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days to ensure even extraction.
After the recommended maceration period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the spent plant material.
Store the finished plantain tincture in a dark glass container with a tight-fitting lid, in a cool, dark location for up to 2 years, using 1-2 drops in the affected eye, 3-4 times daily, as needed.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the plantain plant are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for eye infections due to their anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties.
Seeds: The seeds of the plantain plant contain mucilage, which can help soothe and protect the eyes from infection.
Flowers: The flowers of the plantain plant have antimicrobial properties, making them useful in treating eye infections.
Bark: The bark of the plantain plant contains tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce inflammation in the eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with eye infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can reduce inflammation and fight off infection-causing bacteria. The herb's active compounds, such as flavonoids and alkaloids, have been shown to have antimicrobial effects, making it an effective natural remedy for eye infections. Additionally, yarrow tincture can help soothe irritation and reduce redness, providing relief to individuals suffering from eye infections. Its astringent properties can also help dry up excess fluid and promote healing, making it a valuable addition to any natural medicine cabinet.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of flavonoids like luteolin and apigenin, helps reduce swelling and redness in the eye caused by infection.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of yarrow tincture, attributed to the presence of lactones and essential oils, aids in fighting bacterial and viral infections that cause eye inflammation.
Astringent: Yarrow tincture's astringent property, resulting from tannins, helps tighten and constrict the infected eye tissues, reducing discharge and promoting healing.
Analgesic: The analgesic property of yarrow tincture, owing to the presence of alkaloids, helps alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye infections.

Immune-boosting: Yarrow tincture's immune-boosting property, due to the presence of antioxidants and vitamins, helps strengthen the body's defense mechanism, promoting faster recovery from eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-3 handfuls of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, ensuring they are free from pesticides and pollutants.
Coarsely chop the herbs and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters full.
Pour 80-proof vodka or rum over the herbs until they are completely submerged, then securely fasten the lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid, and store the tincture in a dark glass bottle with a dropper.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antimicrobial properties, making them effective for treating eye infections.
Leaves: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds, helpful in soothing irritated eyes.
Seeds: High in antioxidants, aiding in the prevention of eye damage and infection.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: German chamomile tincture helps with eye infection because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and analgesic properties. The active compounds in chamomile, such as azulene and bisabolol, have been shown to reduce inflammation and irritation in the eyes, making it an effective treatment for eye infections. Additionally, chamomile has been found to have antimicrobial properties that can help to combat the bacteria and viruses that cause eye infections. Furthermore, chamomile can help to alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with eye infections. The tincture form of chamomile allows for easy absorption and quick relief, making it a popular choice for those suffering from eye infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: German chamomile tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to its active constituent chamazulene, helps reduce eye inflammation caused by infection.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property, attributed to the compound azulene and essential oils, aids in fighting bacterial and fungal infections in the eye.
Analgesic: Its analgesic effect, owing to the presence of bisabolol and matricin, helps alleviate pain associated with eye infections.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property, provided by flavonoids and terpenoids, protects the eye from oxidative stress and supports overall eye health.
Immune-boosting: German chamomile tincture's ability to enhance the immune system, facilitated by its essential oils and polysaccharides, assists the body in combating eye infections.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried German chamomile flowers and place them in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or grain alcohol (80-100 proof) over the chopped flowers, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the spent flowers and transferring the liquid to a clean glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and dosage instructions (e.g., 2-3 drops in the affected eye, 3 times a day) and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in therapeutic compounds, chamomile flowers are used for their anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and soothing properties, making them ideal for treating eye infections.
Leaves: Containing various beneficial constituents, chamomile leaves contribute to the medicinal tincture's effectiveness, offering additional support for treating eye infections.
Seeds: Chamomile seeds are a valuable source of therapeutic oils, which can help alleviate inflammation and combat pathogens, further enhancing the tincture's potency against eye infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with eye infection because it has anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and wound-healing properties. The herb contains flavonoids, carotenoids, and volatile oils that have been shown to possess antimicrobial activity against a range of bacteria and fungi. These compounds work together to reduce inflammation, fight infection, and promote healing in the eye. Additionally, pot marigold tincture has been shown to stimulate the production of white blood cells, which helps to boost the immune response and speed up the healing process. Its anti-inflammatory properties also help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eye, providing relief from the symptoms of eye infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tincture helps reduce inflammation in the eye, providing relief from symptoms such as redness, swelling, and pain, due to its active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses responsible for eye infections, thanks to the presence of active compounds like lutein and zeaxanthin.

Antioxidant: The antioxidants in pot marigold tincture, such as vitamin E and carotenoids, protect the eyes from oxidative stress and free radical damage, promoting overall eye health during infection.
Wound-healing: The tincture's wound-healing properties help repair damaged eye tissue and reduce the risk of complications, as it contains active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.

Immune-boosting: Pot marigold tincture supports the immune system, enabling the body to fight off the infection more effectively, as it contains immune-enhancing compounds like polysaccharides and alkaloids.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 handfuls of fresh pot marigold flowers and let them wilt for up to 12 hours to reduce their moisture content.
Chop the wilted flowers and place them in a clean, dry glass jar, filling it up to three-quarters, then pour 80-proof vodka until the flowers are completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve, squeezing out the excess liquid from the marigold flowers.

Transfer the resulting tincture to a dark glass dropper bottle and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Use a few drops in the affected eye, up to three times a day, as needed for eye infection.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for treating eye infections.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have antiseptic and astringent qualities, which help soothe inflamed eyes and fight infection.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds contain essential oils with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, beneficial for treating eye infections and irritations.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with eye infection because it has antibacterial and antiviral properties that can fight off the infection-causing pathogens in the eye. The herb contains hypericin and hyperforin, which have been shown to have antimicrobial effects, making it effective against a range of bacteria and viruses. Additionally, St. John's wort has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eye. Its soothing effects can provide relief from the discomfort and pain associated with eye infections. Furthermore, St. John's wort tincture can also help speed up the healing process, promoting the restoration of healthy eye tissue.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: St. John's wort tincture has the ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria and viruses, which is important for treating eye infections caused by microorganisms. This property is attributed to the presence of hypericin and hyperforin.
Anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's wort tincture helps reduce inflammation, which is a common symptom of eye infections. Hypericin and hyperforin are responsible for this property.

Analgesic properties: St. John's wort tincture has pain-relieving effects, which is important for treating the discomfort caused by eye infections. The active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Antioxidant properties: St. John's wort tincture contains antioxidants, which help protect the eyes from damage caused by oxidative stress. Flavonoids are the active constituents responsible for this property.

Astringent properties: St. John's wort tincture helps to reduce secretions and dry up discharge, which is important for treating eye infections. This property is attributed to the presence of tannins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, ensuring they are fully dried and free from moisture.
Fill a clean jar with the dried herb, filling it up to three-quarters, then pour 100 proof alcohol (or witch hazel extract for an alcohol-free version) until the herb is completely submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for a minimum of two to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
After the designated time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out any excess liquid and discarding the spent herb.
Transfer the resulting tincture to a sterilized glass dropper bottle for easy application and store it in a cool, dark place, where it can last for several years.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, the compounds responsible for St. John's Wort's medicinal properties, making it effective for treating eye infections.
Leaves: Contain similar compounds as flowers, and are often used in combination to increase the potency of medicinal tinctures for eye infection treatment.
Buds: High in antioxidants, buds are used for their potential to support eye health and reduce inflammation in eye infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey tincture helps with eye infection because it contains anti-inflammatory and analgesic properties that can reduce inflammation and alleviate pain in the eyes. It also has antimicrobial and antibacterial properties that can help fight off the infection-causing bacteria, promoting faster healing. Additionally, comfrey tincture contains allantoin, a compound that stimulates the growth of new cells, aiding in the repair of damaged eye tissues. Its anti-inflammatory and healing properties make comfrey tincture an effective natural remedy for eye infections.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tincture's anti-inflammatory property, due to the presence of allantoin, helps reduce swelling and redness in the eye caused by infection.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of comfrey tincture, owing to its rosmarinic acid content, aids in fighting off the bacterial infection in the eye.
Analgesic: Comfrey tincture's analgesic effect, derived from its tannins, helps alleviate pain associated with eye infections.
Wound healing: The wound healing property of comfrey tincture, attributed to allantoin, promotes the healing of damaged eye tissues caused by the infection.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of comfrey tincture, owing to its rosmarinic and caffeic acids, helps protect the eye from oxidative stress and damage caused by the infection.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried comfrey root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 1 pint of vodka or rum over the chopped comfrey, ensuring all pieces are covered.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out any excess liquid.
Store the resulting tincture in a dark glass bottle and use 1-2 drops in the affected eye, up to 3 times daily.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Comfrey leaves are rich in allantoin, a substance that promotes cell growth and healing, making it useful for eye infections.
Flowers: Comfrey flowers contain rosmarinic acid, which has anti-inflammatory properties, making it beneficial for reducing eye inflammation.
Roots: Comfrey roots are high in mucilage, which can help soothe irritated eyes and reduce redness.
Rhizomes: Comfrey rhizomes contain high levels of tannins, which have astringent properties that can help reduce eye secretions and relieve symptoms of eye infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rue tincture helps with eye infection because it has natural antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce inflammation and fight off the bacteria or viruses causing the infection. The herb rue contains alkaloids and essential oils that have been shown to have broad-spectrum antimicrobial activity, making it effective against a range of pathogens that can cause eye infections. Additionally, rue tincture can help to stimulate the immune system, which can assist the body in fighting off the infection and promoting healing. Its astringent properties can also help to reduce redness and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from symptoms of eye infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Rue tincture's antimicrobial properties help combat bacterial and viral infections causing eye irritation, thanks to its active constituents like methyl nonyl ketone and alkaloids.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties reduce eye inflammation and redness, as it contains flavonoids like rutin and quercetin.
Antioxidant: Rue tincture's antioxidant properties protect the eyes from oxidative stress and damage, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Analgesic: The analgesic properties of rue tincture help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with eye infections, as it includes various alkaloids and essential oils.
Astringent: Rue tincture's astringent properties aid in reducing eye secretions and soothing irritation, due to the presence of tannins.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh rue herbs, filling a clean jar with roughly 2 cups of chopped leaves and flowers, then bruise them gently to release their oils.
Pour 100 proof vodka or grain alcohol (enough to cover the herbs by at least 2 inches) into the jar, ensuring all plant material is submerged.
Seal the jar and store it in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking it daily to ensure proper extraction.
After the specified time, strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the plant material.

Store the finished rue tincture in a tightly sealed glass container, away from direct sunlight, for up to 2 years. For eye infection use, consult a healthcare professional for proper dosage and application.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, rue flowers can help reduce inflammation and fight bacterial infections in the eyes.
Leaves: High in antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds, rue leaves are often used to treat eye infections and irritations.
Seeds: Containing therapeutic oils, rue seeds can help alleviate eye inflammation and promote healing in eye infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern arborvitae tincture helps with eye infection because it contains potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are effective in treating various eye conditions. The tincture is derived from the leaves and twigs of the Thuja occidentalis tree, which have been used in traditional Eastern medicine for centuries. The active compounds in the tincture, such as thujone and flavonoids, have been shown to have antimicrobial effects against bacteria and viruses that cause eye infections. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory properties of the tincture help reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from symptoms of eye infections. Overall, eastern arborvitae tincture is a natural and effective remedy for treating eye infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial properties: Eastern arborvitae tincture contains compounds like thujone and isothujone, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of bacteria, making it effective in treating eye infections caused by bacterial agents.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The tincture has active constituents, such as flavonoids and tannins, that can help reduce inflammation in the eye, providing relief from symptoms associated with eye infections.

Antioxidant properties: Eastern arborvitae tincture contains antioxidants, such as terpenes and phenolic acids, which help protect the eye from oxidative stress and damage, promoting overall eye health during infection.

Analgesic properties: The tincture's pain-relieving properties, due to compounds like methyl thujate and isothujone, help alleviate discomfort and pain caused by eye infections, enhancing patient comfort during the healing process.

Immunostimulant properties: Eastern arborvitae tincture can help boost the immune system, thanks to its polysaccharides and essential oils, enhancing the body's ability to fight off eye infections and promoting a quicker recovery.
remedy_recipe: Harvest young eastern arborvitae (Thuja occidentalis) twigs, collecting 2 ounces of fresh foliage.
Chop twigs finely and place in a clean glass jar, adding 1 cup of 100-proof vodka as a menstruum.
Securely seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-4 weeks, shaking daily.
After sufficient maceration, strain the tincture through cheesecloth, squeezing out the marc.
Store the finished arborvitae tincture in a dark glass bottle, keeping it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain active compounds that have antimicrobial properties, making them effective in treating eye infections.
Twigs: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory agents, which can help soothe and heal irritated eyes.
Bark: Has tannins that can reduce inflammation and fight bacteria, making it useful for treating eye infections.
Seeds: Contain essential oils with antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate eye infections.
Buds: High in antioxidants and other beneficial compounds, which can help protect the eyes from infection and promote healing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with eye infection because it contains a high amount of silica, a mineral that is essential for maintaining the health of connective tissues, including those in the eyes. This mineral has anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties that can help reduce inflammation and fight off infection-causing bacteria in the eyes. Additionally, horsetail tincture is rich in antioxidants that can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals. Its astringent properties can also help reduce excess fluid and mucus in the eyes, providing relief from symptoms such as redness, itching, and watering. Overall, horsetail tincture can be a natural and effective remedy for treating eye infections.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial activity: Horsetail tincture contains compounds like phenolic acids and flavonoids that can help inhibit the growth of microorganisms causing eye infections.
Astringent properties: The astringent effect of horsetail tincture, due to tannins, helps reduce inflammation and soothe irritated eyes.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Horsetail tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, partly due to its silica content, which can help alleviate symptoms of eye inflammation.
Antioxidant activity: Horsetail tincture contains antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, that help protect the eyes from oxidative damage and support overall eye health.

Diuretic properties: Horsetail tincture's diuretic effect, attributed to its silica and saponin content, can help reduce fluid retention and relieve pressure in the eyes, which is beneficial for certain eye conditions.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried horsetail herb into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum over the herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solid material.
Label the bottle with the contents, date, and recommended dosage (3-5 drops in the affected eye, 3 times daily).
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes contain silica and other compounds with anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and astringent properties, making them useful for treating eye infections.
Stems: Horsetail stems have high silica content, which can help reduce inflammation and promote healing in eye infections.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves contain antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds that can help alleviate symptoms of eye infections.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat eye infection with herbal tinctures is by using a carefully selected combination of antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory herbs. Eyebright (Euphrasia officinalis), goldenseal (Hydrastis canadensis), and calendula (Calendula officinalis) are some of the most effective herbs for this purpose. Soak a clean cloth in a solution of one teaspoon of the tincture mixture in a cup of boiled, cooled water. Apply the compress to the affected eye for 10-15 minutes, 3-4 times a day. Remember to always consult with a healthcare professional before starting any new treatment.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to eye infection that are treated with herbal tinctures include blepharitis and conjunctivitis. Blepharitis is an inflammation of the eyelids, often caused by bacteria or a skin disorder, and can be treated with goldenseal or calendula tinctures. Conjunctivitis, or pink eye, is an inflammation of the conjunctiva, the membrane that lines the eyelid and eye surface, and can be treated with eyebright or chamomile tinctures. These herbs have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and astringent properties that can help soothe and heal irritated eye tissues. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
